sourceMovie.title: Macross: Do You Remember Love?
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Macross: Do You Remember Love? works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Macross: Do You Remember Love? clicked for you, Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Macross: Do You Remember Love??
faq.answer: Try Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance, Appleseed Alpha, Appleseed—each shares DNA with Macross: Do You Remember Love? while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Macross: Do You Remember Love??
faq.answer: Macross: Do You Remember Love? (1984) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction, War, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Macross: Do You Remember Love? part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Macross Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
